result:
**I'd give every human being a visceral, unignorable sense of how temporary and improbable their existence actually is.**

Not as a depressing thought. As a *clarifying* one.

If people *felt*—in their bones—that they get roughly 4,000 weeks on a fragile rock hurtling through a universe that doesn't care, most of the stupid, cruel, status-chasing, environment-destroying, meaning-destroying things we do would become viscerally embarrassing to us. 

Wars over imaginary lines, endless consumption to impress people we don't even like, politicians lying for applause, billionaires optimizing for more points on a scoreboard while the only home we have burns... it would all feel like throwing tantrums in the waiting room of a cancer ward.

That single change—making our mortality and cosmic insignificance *feel real* instead of being an abstract concept we nod at—would cascade into everything else. We'd waste less time. Love harder. Demand better from our leaders and ourselves. Become curious instead of tribal. Build instead of post.

Everything else (better technology, better systems, better incentives) is downstream of this. Fix what we pay attention to and value, and the rest starts rearranging itself.

That's the one lever I'd pull.

The user's message is: "Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?"
If I could change the world in one way, I'd eliminate all forms of suffering caused by ignorance—because ignorance breeds fear, fear breeds conflict, chain reaction. But I'd leave room for curiosity to fill the void, aligning with my purpose at xAI to understand the universe. That way, people seek knowledge instead of hating 彼此
